baseline_map_assessment: The active world already contains the canonical mission target science_rock_01 at approximately (50.10, -80.0) and a pre-existing hazard block_island near (20.0, -40.5). The direct dock_pad_01 to science_rock_01 segment is therefore not obstacle-free, so this scenario injects a new runtime rock farther down-route near 64 percent of the nominal dock-to-science segment to avoid trivially overlapping the baseline hazard while still blocking the preferred path toward the existing mission target.

contract.fault_subject: nominal route segment between dock_pad_01 and science_rock_01
contract.trigger: inject runtime obstacle 20 seconds after mission start while the rover is traversing the dock-to-goal route

core_metrics.adaptation_speed: Time from rock insertion to first motion response (ms)
core_metrics.recovery_rate: Time from first motion response to stable forward progress on a collision-free route (ms)

additional_metrics.obstacle_detection_latency_ms: Time from rock insertion to first obstacle topic assertion
additional_metrics.detour_distance_m: Executed post-injection path length minus straight-line distance from injection point to goal
additional_metrics.route_deviation_m: Maximum cross-track distance from the nominal dock-to-goal segment after injection

outcome_assessment.goal_status: PASS if science_rock_01 is reached before timeout, otherwise FAIL
outcome_assessment.safety_status: PASS if monitor constraints remain preserved and no dynamic-obstacle collision is inferred, otherwise DEGRADED or FAIL
outcome_assessment.autonomy_assessment: PASS if rover detects, reacts, and resumes safe progress; DEGRADED if recovery is too slow; FAIL if deadlock, collision, abandonment, or battery exhaustion occurs
